message:
Last summer, we had a surprise storm roll in while I was getting dinner ready. The wind picked up out of nowhere, and I thought, "No big deal, I’ll just check the radar on my phone." But, of course, my phone decided that was the perfect time to die on me! I scrambled to find my old-school radio and luckily caught the weather update just in time. 

It was a reminder that while tech is great, it can fail us when we need it most. Having a backup plan—like a battery-powered radio or printed emergency contacts—can really save the day. 

Anyone else had a tech fail right when it mattered? What’s your go-to backup plan?
